name: Barri Office
description_short: Barri is a money transfer and financial services platform offering check cashing, bill payment, mobile recharges, and personal loans through 30+ years of remittance experience.
description_long:
Barri is an established financial services company with over 30 years of experience in international money transfers and remittances. The company operates a hybrid model combining physical store locations with a mobile application to serve primarily Spanish-speaking and Latin American diaspora communities in the United States.

Barri's core service offerings include check cashing with competitive rates and extended hours, international money transfers to Mexico, Central America, and South America, utility and bill payment services, mobile phone recharges across the US and Latin America, and personal loans ranging from $300 to $10,000 through a partnership with Oportun. The company has invested significantly in digital infrastructure, offering a native mobile application for iOS and Android that allows customers to conduct most transactions remotely.

Barri differentiates itself through its combination of physical accessibility (store network) and digital convenience (proprietary app), positioning itself as a one-stop financial hub rather than a single-service provider. The company emphasizes ease of use and customer service, with verified app ratings of 4.9 stars and customer testimonials highlighting reliability and responsive support. Their partnership with Oportun for loan products extends credit-building opportunities to underbanked populations.

Barri operates in a competitive remittance and check-cashing market dominated by larger players. While the company offers legitimate services with reasonable rates and extended hours, customers should note that check-cashing fees, while described as "convenient," are typical industry charges. The loan product is a partnership arrangement rather than direct lending, meaning terms and rates are determined by Oportun. The Spanish-language primary positioning, while serving a specific community well, may limit accessibility for non-Spanish speakers.
address: 3310 S Zarzamora St suite b, San Antonio, TX 78225

company_info.headquarters: 3310 S Zarzamora St suite b, San Antonio, TX 78225

pros: Extended store hours for check cashing with transparent, competitive fee structure
Integrated mobile app enabling remote money transfers, bill payment, and account management without store visits
Partnership with Oportun provides credit-building personal loans from $300–$10,000 for customers with limited credit history
Nationwide mobile recharge service across US and Latin America through single platform
Proven track record with 30+ years of remittance industry experience and high customer satisfaction (4.9-star app rating)
Multi-country reach for transfers to Mexico, Central America, and South America with same-day online options
Debit Visa card offering secure money management and direct deposit capabilities
cons: Check-cashing and money transfer fees not explicitly detailed on homepage; customers must visit locations or app for specific pricing
Loan product is a third-party partnership (Oportun) rather than direct lending, limiting Barri's control over terms and approval rates
Primary marketing and support in Spanish may create accessibility barriers for English-only speakers
No mention of FDIC insurance, fraud protection guarantees, or detailed security certifications for stored funds
Limited transparency about regulatory oversight, licensing, or compliance certifications on public website
best_for: Spanish-speaking immigrants and Latin American diaspora sending remittances to family abroad
Unbanked or underbanked consumers needing check cashing, bill payment, and mobile recharge in one platform
Individuals with thin or no credit history seeking small personal loans to build creditworthiness
Mobile-first users comfortable conducting financial transactions via app rather than in-store
diagnosis: Barri is best suited for Spanish-speaking and Latin American diaspora communities needing reliable, convenient check cashing, remittances, and basic financial services without requiring traditional bank accounts. The main caveat is that specific fee structures and loan terms are not transparently disclosed upfront on the website, and the loan product depends entirely on Oportun's underwriting and pricing—customers should verify all costs before committing.
services: International money transfers to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Mobile phone recharges in US and Latin America
Barri Visa debit card issuance and management
Online and app-based money transfer ordering
In-store financial services at physical locations
Mobile app for account management and transactions
